heading: View Connections in the Connection Registry
body:
To view a connection to your stream processing workspace:

**Manage-connection-cli:**

Atlas CLI

To list all connections from one stream processing workspace you specify using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas streams connections list [options]

```

To return the details for one stream processing connection you specify using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas streams connections describe <streamConnectionName> [options]

```

To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas streams connections list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-streams-connections-list) and
[atlas streams connections describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-streams-connections-describe).

Related Links

- [Install the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/install-atlas-cli)

- [Connect to the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/connect-atlas-cli)

**Manage-connection-ui:**

Atlas UI

- Locate the overview panel of the stream processing workspace containing the
  connection registry you want to view.

- Click Manage.

- Select the Connection Registry tab.

Atlas Stream Processing displays all of the connections available in your
instance connection registry.

**Manage-connection-api:**

Atlas Administration API

The Atlas Administration API provides different endpoints for
retrieving one or all connections in a connection registry.

[View One Connection in a Connection Registry](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-getgroupstreamconnection)

[List All Connections in a Connection Registry](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-listgroupstreamconnections)

**Manage-connection-sh:**

`mongosh`

To list all available connections using `mongosh`, use the
`sp.listConnections()` method. It returns a list of documents
describing each connection in the stream processing workspace's connection
registry. It has the following syntax:

```sh
sp.listConnections()
```
